title: CANN TBE Dependency Pre-flight: Install decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, protobuf Before CANN Operator Execution
symptom: Cryptic ImportError or AttributeError tracebacks from CANN TBE packages (te, tbe, topi) during operator execution on Ascend NPU. The traceback points into site-packages/te/, site-packages/tbe/, or site-packages/topi/ with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'decorator' (or attrs, psutil, cffi, protobuf). Without pre-flight installation, each missing dep requires a separate pip install + retry cycle, costing 5+ iterations in the Phase 5 validation loop.
root_cause: CANN TBE (Tensor Boost Engine) and TE (Tik Engine) packages import decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, and protobuf at runtime during operator compilation and execution without declaring them as install_requires in their setup.py or pyproject.toml. The CANN installation guide assumes these packages are pre-installed, an assumption that breaks in user-managed virtual environments created by SEAM's Phase 2.
fix_steps: 1. Run pre-flight pip install to install all known CANN TBE transitive dependencies in one shot: .venv/bin/pip install decorator attrs psutil cffi protobuf
2. Verify all transitive deps are importable with the one-liner checker: .venv/bin/python -c "[__import__(m) for m in ['decorator','attrs','scipy','psutil','cffi','protobuf']]"
3. Add the transitive deps to the project's requirements.txt or a dedicated cann_tbe_deps.txt so they are installed during Phase 2 venv setup
4. Treat the first ModuleNotFoundError from a TBE import as a dependency issue, not an operator or toolkit issue — always run the pre-flight checker first
affected_patterns: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'decorator' in site-packages/te/ or site-packages/tbe/ traceback
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'attrs' during CANN operator registration
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'psutil' during TBE operator compilation
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'cffi' during TE kernel execution
ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'protobuf' during TBE IR serialization
ImportError traceback pointing into CANN toolkit site-packages (te/, tbe/, topi/)

code_changes.file: requirements.txt (project root, addition for CANN TBE transitive deps)
code_changes.before: # Project dependencies (no CANN TBE transitive deps listed)
code_changes.after:
# CANN TBE transitive dependencies (not declared by CANN whl packages, imported at runtime)
decorator>=5.1.0
attrs>=23.1.0
psutil>=5.9.0
cffi>=1.15.0
protobuf>=4.21.0
scipy>=1.11.0

code_changes.file: .venv pre-flight verdict checker (one-liner, Phase 2 or Phase 5)
code_changes.before: # No pre-flight check — missing deps discovered one at a time via ImportError retry cycles
code_changes.after:
# Pre-flight checker: verify all CANN TBE transitive deps are importable in one shot
.venv/bin/python -c "[__import__(m) for m in ['decorator','attrs','scipy','psutil','cffi','protobuf']]"

references: https://gitee.com/ascend/ModelZoo-PyTorch — Ascend official PyTorch model zoo (reference for dependency patterns)
antipatterns: Do NOT assume that installing torch_npu or ascend-toolkit via pip will pull in decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, or protobuf — these are NOT declared as install_requires by CANN whl packages.

Do NOT treat the first ModuleNotFoundError from a TBE import as an operator or toolkit issue — always run the pre-flight checker first to rule out missing transitive deps before diving into operator debugging.
Do NOT install these deps one at a time in a reactive retry loop — pre-flight installation eliminates 5+ unnecessary pip install + retry cycles in Phase 5 validation.
Do NOT skip scipy in the pre-flight check — some topi operator implementations import scipy at runtime and will fail silently with misleading error messages.

meta.verified_on_model: CANN TBE operator execution on Ascend NPU
meta.refiner_reasoning: This is a fundamental packaging deficiency in CANN TBE whl distributions — the runtime imports decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, and protobuf are not declared as install_requires, causing mandatory iterative pip install + retry cycles for every project that uses CANN TBE operators. The pre-flight installation approach eliminates 5+ retry cycles in Phase 5 validation. This skill applies to ALL Ascend NPU projects that execute CANN TBE operators, making it one of the highest-leverage dependency fixes in the SEAM migration pipeline.

classifier.reasoning: This candidate describes a deterministic dependency pre-flight procedure that eliminates a class of cryptic ImportError/AttributeError failures caused by CANN TBE packages importing decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, and protobuf at runtime without declaring them as install_requires. The fix is a single pip install command followed by a one-liner import checker — both executable by dependency_fixer in Phase 2 (venv creation) and Phase 5 (validation retry loop). Without this pre-flight check, each missing dep causes a separate pip install + retry cycle, costing 5+ iterations. The 0.95 confidence reflects the deterministic nature: if the transitive deps are installed, the TBE ImportError class is eliminated entirely.

problem_description: CANN TBE (Tensor Boost Engine) and TE (Tik Engine) packages import decorator, attrs, psutil, cffi, and protobuf at runtime during operator compilation and execution without declaring them as install_requires. This causes cryptic ImportError or AttributeError tracebacks during CANN operator execution — errors that look like missing operator implementations or toolkit misconfiguration but are in fact just missing transitive Python dependencies. Without pre-flight installation, each missing dep requires a separate pip install + retry cycle, costing 5+ iterations in the Phase 5 validation loop.
rough_fix_approach: 1) Run a single pre-flight pip install command to install all known CANN TBE transitive dependencies in one shot: .venv/bin/pip install decorator attrs psutil cffi protobuf. 2) Verify all transitive deps are importable with a one-liner checker: .venv/bin/python -c "[__import__(m) for m in ['decorator','attrs','scipy','psutil','cffi','protobuf']]". 3) Add the transitive deps to the project's requirements.txt or a dedicated cann_tbe_deps.txt so they are installed during Phase 2 venv setup. 4) Treat the first ModuleNotFoundError from a TBE import as a dependency issue, not an operator or toolkit issue.
name: cann-tbe-dependency-preflight
